q: Atoms of elements are usually found by themselves in nature.
The attractive forces that hold atoms together in compounds are also known as chemical bonds.
Chemical bonds also play an important role in all living things.
The outershell electrons are known as valence electrons.
According to Aufbu principle, electrons are filled in the higher energy atomic orbitals before filling lower energy ones.
The order of filling the orbitals is given as $$1\text{s } 2\text{s } 2\text{p } 3\text{s } 3\text{p } 3\text{d } 4\text{s } 4\text{p}$$.
According to Aufbu principle, two electrons with the same spin cannot occupy the same orbital.
Two electrons in the same orbital must have the same spin.
For an atom, the number of valence electrons is equal to its group number in the Periodic Table.
The octet rule does not apply to $$\text{p}$$-block elements.
A chemical bond is formed when atoms are held together by the repulsive forces between them.
The strength of sharing or transferring the valence electrons depends in the electronegativity of the atoms.
Electronegativity ($$\text{EN}$$) is a measure of the ability of an atom to attract the shared electrons to itself.
The greater an element's electronegativity, the stronger its attractive power to electrons will be.
The elements having low electron affinity easily gain electrons, resulting in the formation of anions.
The elements having high ionization energy easily lose electrons, resulting in the formation of the cations.
The higher the first ionization energy of an element, the greater is its electronegativity.
The higher the value of the electronegativity, the less is the atom's attraction to electrons.
There are two main types of chemical bonding, such as ionic bonding and covalent bonding.
The atoms within molecules are kept together stronger forces.
The forces between molecules are weak.
Intermolecular forces are hydrogen bonding and van der Waals forces such as ion-dipole interaction, dipole-dipole interaction, and London dispersion forces.
In general, an ionic bond is not as strong as a covalent bond.
The hydrogen bond is the strongest intermolecular force followed by Van der Waals forces.
Among the Van der Waals forces, London dispersion forces are strongest intermolecular forces that can be found in all substances.
Ionic bonding does not depend on the difference in electronegativity between two atoms.
When the difference in electronegativity is greater than 1.6, ionic compounds are formed.
Ionic bonding generally occurs between reactive metals and reactive non-metals.
When ionic bond forms, a metal loses electrons to form negatively charged ions.
The number of lost or gained electrons by an atom during ionic bonding is the same as the valency of that element.
In ionic compounds, the cations and anions are held together by weak intermolecular electrostatic attraction.
The greater the charge in the ions, the greater the electrostatic attraction and the stronger the covalent bond will be.
The melting points of the ionic compounds with the greater charge on the ions are lower.
In solid ionic compounds, their ions are held in fixed positions and they can conduct electricity.
An ionic bond is formed by sharing one or more pairs of valence electrons between atoms.
The covalent bond is the electrostatic attraction between the nuclei of the atoms and the shared electrons.
Covalent bonding usually occurs between metals.
A double covalent bond is formed by sharing two pair electrons between two atoms.
A triple covalent bond is formed by sharing three pair electrons between two atoms.
The length of the bond is determined by the number of unshared electrons.
The higher the bonded electrons, the stronger the attraction between the two atoms.
The higher the bonded electrons, the shorter the bond length.
The bond length of the single bond is the shortest.
The bond strength of the triple bond is the strongest.
A coordinate bond is not a special type of covalent bond.
A coordinate bond is formed when one of the participant atoms possesses a lone pair electron.
The chemical forming between $$\text{Be}$$ and $$\text{Cl}$$ is the ionic bonding.
Covalent bonding occurs only by transferring valence electrons between the atoms involved in the bond.
To transfer a valence electron, the atom must have higher electronegativity than another atom.
The molecular shape of water molecule, $$\text{H}_2\text{O}$$, is linear.
Beryllium chloride, $$\text{BeCl}_2$$, is a stable molecule that follows the octet rule.
Carbon tetrachloride is a polar covalent molecule.
The London dispersion forces are instantaneous dipole-dipole interaction.
The London dispersion forces exists only in polar molecules.
Metals can conduct electricity easily due to a sea of electrons.
$$\text{HCN}$$ is a polar covalent molecule.
$$\text{CO}$$ is a non-polar covalent molecule.
The shape of ammonia is V-shaped.
Graphite can conduct electricity.
In quartz, each silicon atom is bonded to four oxygen atoms.
The melting of some metals become lower compared within the ionic compounds.
The strength of a metallic bond is higher than that of an ionic bond.
Energy changes in chemical reactions are studied under the branch of energy.
Photosynthesis is the process by which green plants convert light energy into chemical energy.
Energy stored in chemical bonds is known as potential energy.
Nuclear energy is harnessed in nuclear reactors to generate electricity.
Energy can be transformed from one form to another, but it cannot be created or destroyed.
Energy associated with the motion of an object is potential energy.
Energy stored due to an object's position or composition is kinetic energy.
A process that occurs without outside intervention is called spontaneous.
Batteries convert electrical energy into chemical energy.
In a light bulb, electrical energy is converted into light and heat.
Food provides chemical energy for the body to function.
Solar panels convert light energy from the sun into electricity.
Physical and chemical changes are always accompanied by energy changes.
The law of conservation of energy maintains the overall energy of the universe.
Chemical reactions involve the breaking and forming of bonds.
During an endothermic reaction, the energy of the system will decrease.
The sun is the primary source of energy for Earth.
The process of digestion breaks down food to release energy.
Glucose and other carbohydrates are the main energy sources for cells.
For an exothermic reaction, the change in enthalpy ($$\Delta\text{H}$$) is positive.
The SI unit for energy is the Joule ($$\text{J}$$).
Heat is the form of energy that flows between two objects due to a temperature difference.
One calorie is equal to 4.184 Joules.
Specific heat is an intensive property.
Standard enthalpy of formation for elements in their stable state is zero.
Hess's Law states that the total enthalpy change depends on the path taken.
A closed system can exchange matter with its surroundings.
An isolated system can exchange neither energy nor matter.
Thermodynamics is the study of heat and its relationship to energy.
Internal energy of a system is the sum of kinetic and potential energies of its particles.
Entropy ($$\text{S}$$) is a measure of molecular randomness.
Gibbs free energy change ($$\Delta\text{G}$$) determines reaction spontaneity.
At constant pressure, heat change is equal to enthalpy change.
An exothermic reaction feels cold to the touch.
Combustion of methane is an endothermic process.
The units of specific heat are $$\text{J g}^{-1} \text{K}^{-1}$$.
Work done by the system on surroundings is taken as positive.
The first law of thermodynamics is $$\Delta\text{U} = \text{q} + \text{w}$$.
